title: coreopsis glaucodes
intro: Coreopsis glaucodes, also known as Rough-seed Coreopsis, is a perennial wildflower native to the Great Plains region of North America. The medicinal properties of Coreopsis glaucodes are attributed to its roots, flowers, and leaves, which have been used in herbal remedies to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, sore throats, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Coreopsis glaucodes can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are said to provide relief from fever, headaches, and digestive issues.
